title: Sebastiania ramosissima
taxonomy: Sebastiania ramosissima is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is native to South America, where it can be found in various habitats, including dry forests and grasslands. The plant is a shrub or small tree with a distinctive growth habit, characterized by a dense network of branches and stems. Sebastiania ramosissima is a member of the genus Sebastiania, which consists of approximately 20 species of plants native to the Americas. Within the Euphorbiaceae family, Sebastiania ramosissima is part of the subfamily Acalyphoideae.
